info.title: The ReliefWeb API
info.description:
Interactive API documentation for the data API of Reliefweb.int. ReliefWeb is a leading source of information on global crises and disasters.

Our continuous coverage and archive going back to the 1970s makes ReliefWeb an unparalleled resource for both current and historical data on humanitarian response.

The vast majority of content on ReliefWeb has been contributed by humanitarian partners. We owe it to the community to make data more accessible and reusable, so that developers can build tools that increase exposure for valuable content and facilitate analysis for better decisions.

tags.name: Reports
tags.description: Gateway to updates and analysis from more than 4,000 global information sources.

get.description: Review location and status of all versions.
Review list of main entrypoints and top-level resources.

get.summary: List or search updates, headlines or maps.

parameters.name: query[value]
parameters.description: Specify the terms of your fuzzy search. The default is no query.

parameters.name: query[operator]
parameters.description: Specify the operator by which your search query words will be combined.

parameters.name: query[fields][]
parameters.description: Specify which fields to target with your search. You may use boost syntax here, see the Advanced API Usage documentation.

parameters.name: filter[field]
parameters.description: Field to filter by. Every condition must contain either a field or conditions property.

parameters.name: filter[value][]
parameters.description: Value for the field being filtered. Leave blank to require existence of the field, must be present for range and value conditions.

parameters.name: filter[operator]
parameters.description: Operator by which filters will be combined.

parameters.name: filter[negate]
parameters.description: Reverse the filter to include those items that do not match.

parameters.name: filter[conditions][]
parameters.description: This property is used to combine conditions with a logical connector (the operator property). Every condition must contain one of the field or conditions properties.

parameters.name: facets[field]
parameters.description: Field to facet by. May include dates, references or the status field.

parameters.name: facets[name]
parameters.description: Identifier for resulting facet data. Useful when faceting multiple ways on the same field.

parameters.name: facets[limit]
parameters.description: Limit the number of facets returned for non-date based facets.

parameters.name: facets[sort]
parameters.description: The sort order of non-date facets. Sorting may be by value or count of terms, and ascending or descending. count defaults to desc, value defaults to asc.

parameters.name: facets[filter][]
parameters.description: Apply all the same capabilities of the filter parameter to the specific results of this facet request.

parameters.name: facets[interval]
parameters.description:
Round date information to the nearest interval unit.
Apply all the same capabilities of the filter parameter to the specific results of this facet request.

parameters.name: fields[include][]
parameters.description: Specify which fields to include for each item. Defaults to an item label.

parameters.name: fields[exclude][]
parameters.description: Specify sub-fields to exclude from overall results. For example, if you include date you may exclude date.changed.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return for a list query. The default is 10 and the maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The offset from which to return the items in a list query. Allows paging through all results. The default is 0.

parameters.name: sort[]
parameters.description: Sort the list results. Defaults to search relevance. Values in the form of fieldname:(asc|desc).

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Pre-built standard selections of fields to return for ease of displaying lists or items.

parameters.name: preset
parameters.description: Pre-built standard configurations of queries, filters and sorts for common use cases.
